plain_definition: A category of stablecoin-like crypto-asset under MiCA Title III that tries to hold a stable value by referencing more than one thing -- for example a basket of currencies, commodities, or other crypto-assets -- rather than a single official currency (which is instead an e-money token). Offering an ART to the public in the EU, or seeking its admission to trading, requires authorisation by a national competent authority and an NCA-approved crypto-asset white paper, plus ongoing reserve-of-assets, redemption-at-market-value, and own-funds requirements on the issuer. Title III has applied since 30 June 2024, six months ahead of the rest of MiCA. This is the EU's own regime-specific instrument in the same space as this glossary's general 'stablecoin' entry -- broadly the EU counterpart to what Hong Kong's Stablecoins Ordinance and the UK's qualifying-stablecoin regime cover for their own currencies.
formal_definition: MiCA's own EUR-Lex summary describes asset-referenced tokens as 'crypto-assets that stabilise their value in relation to other assets or a basket of assets'; the formal definition sits in MiCA Article 3(1)(6). Title III (Articles 16-47) sets authorisation, white-paper, reserve-of-assets, redemption and own-funds requirements for ART issuers, applicable since 30 June 2024 (Regulation (EU) 2023/1114, OJ L 150, 9.6.2023).
